meta.mode: PUBLIC-SAFE SSR REMEDIATION SEQUENCING — analysis only, no runtime change, no wrapper, no centralization, no migration
meta.scope: Exactly the 17 public-safe files from H2.6. Protected/excluded/never-touch domains are out of scope.
meta.note: Sequencing is advisory for a FUTURE, separately-authorized phase. Nothing is remediated, wrapped, centralized, or migrated here. Crisis routing & business↔healing separation preserved.

ssrBlockerType: bare localStorage access without typeof-window guard
files.smallestAdditiveRemediationPattern: Wrap the access in a `typeof window !== 'undefined'` guard, or move the read into useEffect.
files.reversibility: high (additive guard / two-pass init; revert by restoring original line or checkpoint)
files.migrationReadinessAfterRemediation: Becomes a qualified lowest-blast-radius stabilization candidate (H2.5 gates) once SSR + hydration guards are added.
files.rollbackComplexity: trivial (restore single guarded line; or checkpoint rollback)
verificationRequirements: npm run verify:all green (build + scans)
route still returns 200
no React hydration warning in console (server/client parity)
storage read/write value parity (persisted value identical before/after)
behavior parity: storage-available path unchanged; storage-unavailable path degrades gracefully (no throw)
reduced-motion / a11y unaffected on the page
whyLowRisk: additive guard / two-pass init only — no API or data-shape change

hydrationBlockerType: storage read inside useState initializer (server/client value mismatch)
files.smallestAdditiveRemediationPattern: Move the initial read out of the initializer: default-init state, then hydrate via useEffect + setState after mount (two-pass).
files.reversibility: high (additive guard / two-pass init; revert by restoring original line or checkpoint)
files.migrationReadinessAfterRemediation: Becomes a qualified lowest-blast-radius stabilization candidate (H2.5 gates) once SSR + hydration guards are added.
files.rollbackComplexity: trivial (restore single guarded line; or checkpoint rollback)
verificationRequirements: npm run verify:all green (build + scans)
